summary: Verified auto-verify system fix. The auto-verify now correctly requests localhost:3000/ (the actual page) instead of /api/health, which forces Next.js to recompile all imports and returns 500 or Build Error HTML when broken. All unit tests pass (18/18 edit-lines, 4/4 agent-loop-edit-flow). Health check returns healthy. Frontend login and dashboard load correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-verify system fix verified. The system now correctly detects syntax errors by requesting the actual page (localhost:3000/) instead of just the health endpoint.

frontend_login.note: Login with REDACTED_LEAKED_USER / REDACTED_LEAKED_PASSWORD works, redirects to dashboard

auto_verify_uses_page_request.note: Both patch_files and edit_lines now request the actual page instead of /api/health

locations: Line 2393: patch_files checks for 'Build Error', 'SyntaxError', 'Module build failed', 'Expected', 'Unexpected token'
Line 2733: edit_lines checks for 'Build Error', 'SyntaxError', 'Module build failed', 'Expected', 'Unexpected token'
Line 2443: agentLoopContinue = true (patch_files build failure handler)
retry_loop_for_patch_files.note: Retry loop properly implemented for both patch_files and edit_lines paths

fix_verification.issue: Auto-verify previously hit /api/health which always returned 200 even when page files had syntax errors
fix_verification.fix: Auto-verify now requests localhost:3000/ which forces Next.js to recompile all imports and returns 500 or Build Error HTML when broken
fix_verification.verification_status: VERIFIED - Both patch_files (line 2388) and edit_lines (line 2726) now fetch the main page and check HTML for build errors
